calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; Memo P & A; Dec. And Pos
Off Calendar Sep-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Patricia Wilson Hadfield, Bankruptcy Law Group, 775.827.9600, appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Plaintiff's request for a continuance was Denied. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S, Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is GRANTED. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $360. Plaintiff shall submit a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling by the end of the day on September 24, 2012. Anthony Schoenberg, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-12-2012 Off Calendar

Application For A Prove-Up Hearing Date To Have Default Judgment Entered Against Defts
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Court Trial/Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.
Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. special appearance for plaintiff Creditors Trade Assoc., Inc., dba Collection Bureau.

Plaintiff Witness Gary Looney and Susan Strebeck, were placed under oath and direct examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) Sierra Coast Investments, Inc., a California Corp. and Billy P. Desmond as follows:

Damages/Principal - $92614.44
Prejudgment Interest - $26540.08
Attorney's Fees - per motion.
Costs - $535.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $119716.52

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
